title: hypericum corifolium
intro: Hypericum corifolium, also known as tutsan, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of tutsan include its ability to stimulate wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a popular herb for skin and wound care. Tutsan can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
